settings.title: The Hands Behind the Calm
settings.excerpt: Our lead therapist Mara warms the room and the towels before you ever walk in.
settings.body:
The hush you feel when you step into Velvet Touch does not happen by accident. Mara, who leads our team, sets it up before the first guest arrives.

She is usually here a good while before opening, moving through the rooms in a quiet routine she has kept for years. By the time the door opens, everything is already settled.

The quiet preparation

She lights the room, warms the towels, and checks the music is low enough to disappear. Fifteen years in, she reads tension in a person's shoulders before they say a word.

A few of the small things she insists on:

  • Towels warm, never just out of the cupboard.
  • The room scent gentle, so it soothes rather than announces itself.
  • Lighting soft enough that you can close your eyes without effort.

Tailored, not by the book

Mara trained in both Swedish and deep-tissue work, and she tailors every session to the body on the table. No two of her treatments are quite the same, because no two people arrive carrying the same week.

If you are not sure what to book, ask for her. She will ask a question or two, feel where you are holding tension, and decide with you from there. Most people leave having had exactly what they needed, even if it was not what they came in asking for.
